name: ghcp-mem
description: A local, auditable session-memory layer for VS Code and GitHub Copilot, built for engineers who need persistent project context without relying on a cloud memory backend.

commands.title: GHCP-MEM: Inject Relevant Context Into Copilot Chat...
GHCP-MEM: Show External MCP Client Config
GHCP-MEM: Export Session as Diff-Friendly Markdown...

chatParticipants.name: mem
chatParticipants.description: Query persistent coding session memory with progressive disclosure

commands.name: health
commands.description: Show memory health score and notes

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search past sessions for a keyword/query

commands.name: timeline
commands.description: Show chronological sessions around a date/query

commands.name: detail
commands.description: Get full detail of a session by ID prefix

commands.name: recent
commands.description: Show the most recent sessions

commands.name: azure
commands.description: Show Azure-tagged sessions grouped by subsystem

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a session (by ID prefix) as diff-friendly markdown

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show memory store stats

commands.name: standup
commands.description: Generate an AI daily standup note from yesterday's sessions

commands.name: commit
commands.description: Generate an AI conventional commit message from staged git changes + sessions

commands.name: ask
commands.description: Ask a question about your coding history (RAG over sessions)

commands.name: recap
commands.description: Generate a narrative weekly/monthly recap of your coding sessions

commands.name: savings
commands.description: Show token savings breakdown — compression ratio, lifetime totals, and dollar-equivalent estimates

commands.name: related
commands.description: Find all sessions that touched the currently open file — zero typing needed

commands.name: decisions
commands.description: Export architecture decisions as an ADR-style doc, optionally filtered by keyword

commands.name: whereami
commands.description: Interruption recovery brief — what you were working on, where you left off, and your next step

commands.name: debt
commands.description: Technical debt ledger — surface TODO/FIXME/HACK signals from session history, grouped by age

commands.name: adr
commands.description: Auto-generate a formal Architecture Decision Record from session history, optionally filtered by topic

commands.name: pr
commands.description: PR review context — surface sessions matching a PR's changed files (pass branch name or PR number)

commands.name: precommit
commands.description: Pre-commit architectural consistency check — verify staged changes against past decisions before committing

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Workspace integrity audit — catch version drift and cross-file inconsistencies (e.g. README footer vs package.json)

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Re-run grounding validation on a session by ID prefix — shows verified / drifted / missing files

commands.name: correct
commands.description: Correct a session by ID prefix — captures a new corrected note, links it to the original, and supersedes it

commands.name: supersede
commands.description: Mark one session as superseding another (usage: /supersede <newerId> <olderId>)

commands.name: retract
commands.description: Retract a session by ID prefix — excludes it from retrieval and injection (use 'undo' to reverse)

commands.name: noise
commands.description: Mark a session as low-quality noise — excludes it from injection and retrieval (use 'undo' to reverse)

commands.name: janitor
commands.description: Re-score every stored session against the current quality floor and flag/unflag low-quality memories

commands.name: accept
commands.description: Mark a retrieved session as useful — strengthens its retrieval reinforcement signal

commands.name: reject
commands.description: Mark a retrieved session as unhelpful — weakens its retrieval reinforcement signal

commands.name: entity
commands.description: Aggregate every session that touched a file or symbol (usage: /entity <path> or /entity <path>#<symbol>)

commands.name: snippet
commands.description: Chunk-level retrieval — returns matching decisions/problems/summaries/topics across all sessions

commands.name: conflicts
commands.description: List pending conflict warnings — sessions whose new decisions may contradict older ones

commands.name: lineage
commands.description: Show cross-session causal chain (predecessors + successors) for a session by ID prefix

commands.name: why
commands.description: Score-decomposition explainer — break down why a session ranked where it did (usage: /why <query> :: <id>)

commands.name: graph
commands.description: Emit a Mermaid flowchart of the decision graph (supersession + correction + causal edges); optional file: filter

commands.name: compliance
commands.description: Audit-friendly posture report: grounding coverage, trust distribution, conflict counts, redaction stats, custom-entity inventory

commands.name: route
commands.description: Context-acquisition recommender — given a question, returns whether MCP tools or file open is cheaper and estimates token cost for each path

commands.name: lessons
commands.description: List consolidated semantic + procedural lessons; `add <text>` pins one, `forget <id>` removes one

commands.name: rules
commands.description: List team-shared project rules from .github/memory/rules.md; `add [category:]<text>` adds one, `remove <id>` deletes one

commands.name: pin
commands.description: Return a session evicted with /evict back to the injected working set for this VS Code session

commands.name: evict
commands.description: Drop a session from the injected working set for this VS Code session without deleting it from the store

languageModelTools.name: ghcpMem_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the user's persistent GHCP-MEM coding session memory for past decisions, problems solved, files touched, and topics. Use this BEFORE asking the user for repo context that might already be known. Returns a compact list of matching sessions with IDs, summaries, files, and decisions.

sinceDays.description: Only return sessions from the last N days.

workspaceOnly.description: Limit results to the current workspace (default true).

languageModelTools.name: ghcpMem_store
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Persist a short, self-contained note into GHCP-MEM so it will be recalled in future sessions. Use when the user states a durable fact, decision, or preference the assistant should remember. Do NOT use for ephemeral chat context.

summary.description: 2-4 sentence summary of what should be remembered.

languageModelTools.name: ghcpMem_audit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run GHCP-MEM's workspace integrity audit. Catches cross-file inconsistencies that erode release trust — most importantly, version drift between package.json, README footer, docs/DEMO.md, and CHANGELOG.md. Returns a list of errors and warnings with the offending file, line, and a suggested fix. Read-only — does not modify any file or memory.

rules.description: Optional subset of rules to run (e.g. ['version-drift']). Empty/omitted = run all.

languageModelTools.name: ghcpMem_lessons
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return GHCP-MEM's consolidated semantic and procedural lessons: durable project facts and how-to sequences distilled from recurring decisions across many past sessions, plus any hand-pinned lessons. This is the cheapest, highest-signal memory surface — prefer it over re-reading source when the question is 'what is true about this project' or 'how do we usually do X'. Read-only. Optionally filter by kind (semantic facts or procedural how-tos).

kind.description: Optional filter: 'semantic' for facts, 'procedural' for how-to sequences.

ghcpMem.compressionIntervalMinutes.description: How often to compress captured events (minutes)

ghcpMem.maxStoredSessions.description: Maximum number of compressed sessions to retain

ghcpMem.maxStoreSizeMB.markdownDescription: Soft cap on the on-disk size of `~/.ghcp-mem/sessions.json`. When exceeded, oldest sessions are evicted until under cap (count-based `#ghcpMem.maxStoredSessions#` and age-based `#ghcpMem.retentionDays#` still apply first).

ghcpMem.retentionDays.description: Delete sessions older than this many days (0 = disabled)

ghcpMem.enterpriseMode.markdownDescription: Strict mode for regulated environments. Disables terminal capture, raw snippets, team export, and MCP write tools, and stores only redacted summaries.

ghcpMem.captureCodeSnippets.markdownDescription: Store a short redacted snippet for file edits. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

ghcpMem.allowMcpWriteAccess.markdownDescription: Allow MCP write tools (memory-store, memory-delete, memory-correct, memory-retract, memory-supersede). **Default `false`** — MCP exposes read-only retrieval (search, recent, timeline, get) until you explicitly opt in. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

ghcpMem.allowTeamExport.markdownDescription: Allow team export and memory-pack export flows. Disabled automatically in enterprise mode.

ghcpMem.previewBeforePersist.markdownDescription: Show a preview before a compressed memory snapshot is persisted.

ghcpMem.contextRetrievalCount.description: Number of relevant past sessions to inject

ghcpMem.redactSecrets.description: Scan and redact secrets/PII before storing

ghcpMem.detectHighEntropySecrets.description: Heuristically redact high-entropy tokens (random API keys, base64 credentials, opaque session tokens) that no named redaction rule matched. Lowercase hex digests and git SHAs are spared.

ghcpMem.localEmbeddings.description: Enable dependency-free local (lexical) embeddings for hybrid dense retrieval when the neural vscode.lm embeddings API is unavailable. Runs fully offline with no native dependencies. Disable to use keyword + recency ranking only.

ghcpMem.excludeGlobs.description: Glob patterns whose file events will be skipped

ghcpMem.autoInjectStartupContext.description: Write .github/instructions/session-memory.instructions.md on every VS Code startup and after each compression so Copilot always has recent session context.

ghcpMem.startupContextSessionCount.description: Number of recent sessions to include in the auto-injected instructions file.

ghcpMem.projectRules.description: Inject durable, team-shared project rules from .github/memory/rules.md (managed with @mem /rules) at the top of every Copilot/agent session. Commit that file to share rules with your team.

ghcpMem.idleTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds of editor inactivity before idle-triggered compression fires (0 = disabled)

flags.description: Regex flags (g, i, m, etc.). Defaults to 'g'

ghcpMem.customRedactionRules.description: User-defined regex redaction rules applied after the built-in 26-rule set (enterprise compliance modes)

ghcpMem.customSensitiveEntities.description: Plain-string entity names (organisations, products, codenames, customer aliases) to redact verbatim. Each entry is treated as a case-insensitive, word-boundary-anchored literal — no regex syntax needed. Example: ["AcmeCorp internal", "Project Hydra", "Deal-2026-Q3"].

ghcpMem.autosave.enabled.description: Enable context-pressure autosave (flush based on event count + wall clock)

ghcpMem.autosave.eventThreshold.description: Trigger autosave once this many captured events are buffered

ghcpMem.autosave.minutesThreshold.description: Trigger autosave after this many minutes since the last flush (with any pending events)

ghcpMem.healthAlertThreshold.description: Show a warning notification if memory health score falls below this value (0 = disabled)

ghcpMem.scope.markdownDescription:
Retrieval scope for stored sessions.

- `repo` (default): sessions captured against the same git origin URL (works across machines/clones). Prevents memories leaking between projects when you open a new workspace.
- `workspace`: sessions captured in the current VS Code workspace only.
- `user`: all sessions across all workspaces and repos.

Regardless of this setting, sessions tagged with any value in `#ghcpMem.globalTags#` are always included.

ghcpMem.globalTags.markdownDescription: User tags that promote a session to global scope: it is always included in startup context and retrieval regardless of `#ghcpMem.scope#`. Reserve for cross-repo knowledge such as organization coding standards, naming conventions, or Well-Architected Framework guidance. Tag a session with `GHCP-MEM: Tag Session...`.

ghcpMem.qualityFloor.markdownDescription: Minimum heuristic quality score (0-1) required for a captured session to be injected. Sessions below the floor are flagged `lowQuality`, kept on disk for audit, and excluded from the startup context block. Signals include grounded decisions, summary length, observation type, and event volume. Set to 0 to disable the gate.

ghcpMem.janitorEnabled.markdownDescription: Periodically re-score stored sessions against the current `#ghcpMem.qualityFloor#` and flag/unflag `lowQuality` accordingly.

ghcpMem.janitorIntervalDays.description: How often (in days) the janitor re-scores stored sessions.

ghcpMem.janitorPruneAfterDays.markdownDescription: If > 0, the janitor deletes sessions that have been `lowQuality` for at least this many days AND were never `/accept`-ed. `0` disables pruning (the default — flagging only).

ghcpMem.validateAgainstCodebase.description: Drop or de-rank retrieved memories whose key files no longer exist or are empty in the current codebase.

ghcpMem.freshnessFloor.description: Minimum freshness score (0-1, fraction of key files still present) required for a memory to survive validation. Lower = more lenient.

ghcpMem.githubCompatibleMode.markdownDescription: Mirror GitHub Copilot's agentic-memory contract: 28-day retention and repo-scoped retrieval. When enabled, overrides `#ghcpMem.retentionDays#` and `#ghcpMem.scope#`.

ghcpMem.policySource.markdownDescription: Optional remote URL to a validated JSON array of redaction rules. Loaded on startup and after settings changes, then appended to the built-in policy set.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with GHCP-MEM
walkthroughs.description: Persistent, redacted session memory for GitHub Copilot — set up in four steps.

steps.title: Choose your privacy posture
steps.description:
Run the privacy wizard to lock down capture, snippets, terminal commands, and exports before your first session.
[Run privacy wizard](command:ghcpMem.runPrivacyWizard)

steps.title: Open the Sessions view
steps.description:
GHCP-MEM stores compressed coding sessions in the **Memory** sidebar. Open it to confirm capture is active.
[Open Sessions view](command:workbench.view.extension.ghcpMem)

steps.title: Capture your first snapshot
steps.description:
Force-capture the current activity so you can see how compression and redaction work.
[Capture snapshot](command:ghcpMem.captureSnapshot)

steps.title: Chat with your memory
steps.description:
Open Copilot Chat and type `@mem /recent` to query past sessions. Try `/search`, `/timeline`, `/azure`, `/health`, or `/export`.
[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open)

steps.title: Tune retention & GitHub-compatible mode
steps.description:
Open Settings and adjust **Retention Days**, **Max Stored Sessions**, **Max Store Size MB**, or enable **GitHub Compatible Mode** to mirror Copilot's 28-day repo-scoped contract.
[Open settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22ghcpMem%22)

toolSets.name: GHCP-MEM
toolSets.description: Search and save to GHCP-MEM persistent session memory

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check:release:strict && npm run typecheck && npm run bundle:prod
scripts.test: npm run lint && tsc -p ./tsconfig.test.json && node scripts/setup-test-env.js && node scripts/run-tests.mjs
scripts.measure:activation: npm run bundle:prod && tsc -p ./tsconfig.test.json && node scripts/measure-activation.js
scripts.verify: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run check:release && npm run eval:check && npm run bench && npm run bundle:prod
scripts.package: npm run vscode:prepublish && vsce package --out ghcp-mem.vsix
